sections.paragraph:
κόρος, ου, ὁ (PSI 554, 14 [259 b.c.]; LXX; TestJud 9:8; Eupolemus the Jewish historian [II b.c.]: 723 Fgm. 2, 33 Jac. [in Eus., PE 9, 33]; Joseph.) a Heb. dry measure (כֹּר ‘cor, kor’; HLewy, D. Semit. Fremdwörter im Griech. 1895, 116), a measure of capacity for grain, flour, etc.; acc. to Jos., Ant. 15, 314=ten Attic medimni, hence about 393 liters=about 4 hectoliters, or betw. ten and twelve bushels; measure Lk 16:7.—Lit. under II βάτος.—DELG s.v. 3 κόρος. M-M. TW. Sv.

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Heb. Heb. = Hebrew

acc. to acc. to = according to

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

betw. betw. = between

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 560
